stepAnswer: 70% because it always predicts benign $$=$$ $$1$$, and $$35$$ out of $$50$$ test points are benign $$=$$ $$1$$.
stepTitle: g) Marissa suggests that we use a constant classifier which will always predict the class that is most common in the training set. In our test set, there are $$15$$ benign $$=$$ $$0$$ data points and $$35$$ benign $$=$$ $$1$$ data points. What will the accuracy of the constant classifier be on our test set?
answerLatex: 70% because it always predicts benign $$=$$ $$1$$, and $$35$$ out of $$50$$ test points are benign $$=$$ $$1$$.
choices: 70% because it always predicts benign $$=$$ $$1$$, and $$35$$ out of $$50$$ test points are benign $$=$$ $$1$$.
30% because it only gets the benign $$=$$ $$0$$ points right since it predicts the majority class.
$$66.7\%$$ because it matches the training set ratio of $$\frac{120}{180}$$.
50% because a constant classifier is no better than a fair coin flip.
